parameters.value: ={"query":"mutation UploadContractFile($file: Upload!, $fieldMetadataId: String!) { uploadFilesFieldFile(file: $file, fieldMetadataId: $fieldMetadataId) { id } }","variables":{"file":null,"fieldMetadataId":"{{ $('DocuSign Config').item.json.twentyPullSignedContractFieldId }}"}}

parameters.subject: =DocuSign contract could not be auto-matched
=DocuSign contract PDF download failed
=DocuSign contract processing failed

parameters.content:
## AUTO-12

Triggered by DocuSign Connect on envelope completion. Register this workflow's Production Webhook URL as a DocuSign Connect configuration (Admin â†’ Connect â†’ Add Configuration), event: Envelope Completed.

Reps still send contracts manually via the DocuSign website â€” this workflow only handles the completion side.

Matches signer email to open Pulls (VISITED/OUT/DUE_SOON/OVERDUE). Exactly one match â†’ auto-attach PDF to Pull.signedContract + Person.contracts and set Pull.contractSigned. Zero or multiple matches â†’ email OWNER_EMAIL for manual resolution.

HMAC verification uses DocuSign's standard documented Connect scheme (X-DocuSign-Signature-1, HMAC-SHA256/base64 over raw body) â€” not yet verified against a live DocuSign account's actual payload; revisit if real Connect payloads differ.

All Twenty/DocuSign HTTP nodes after signature verification use onError: continueErrorOutput, routed to "Notify Team - Processing Failed" so no failure is silent. The 200 ack to DocuSign fires immediately after signature verification (before processing completes) since DocuSign expects a fast response â€” processing failures are caught and reported via email instead of via the HTTP response.

**No $env access on this n8n plan** â€” credentials are hardcoded in two places instead: the "DocuSign Config" Set node (Client ID, User ID, Account ID, base URL, RSA private key, webhook secret, Twenty field metadata ID) and inline in "Compute Expected Signature"'s code (webhook secret, needed before Config exists). BEFORE ACTIVATING: fill in every PASTE_*_HERE placeholder in both. DocuSign auth uses JWT Grant (RSA keypair + one-time consent, already completed) â€” "Mint DocuSign JWT" signs a fresh assertion every run, "Exchange JWT for Access Token" trades it for a ~1hr bearer token, no manual token renewal ever needed. Treat this workflow's exported JSON as a secret â€” it will contain live credentials once filled in; do not commit a filled-in copy to git.
